description: Upstream Node Runtime Replacement — replace the current polyfill/bootstrap Node implementation with an upstream-Node-shaped runtime. Start with pre-research on bootstrap-critical bindings, make upstream fs the first real module target, compile the backend through the existing WasmVM path, and then bring modules up one by one.

userStories.title: Build host-side pre-research probes for bootstrap-critical bindings
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need a small host-side probe harness so we can verify the riskiest upstream Node bindings before committing to the rebuild.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a focused probe harness under packages/nodejs/ or packages/secure-exec/tests/ that can execute upstream bootstrap assets without requiring the full replacement runtime first
Probe internal/bootstrap/realm and internal/bootstrap/node with the narrowest viable binding set
Add a module_wrap probe that compiles and executes a trivial ESM module or documents the exact blocker
Add a contextify probe that exercises node:vm createContext()/runInContext() or documents the exact blocker
Add uv and cares_wrap probes that exercise a minimal net listen/connect path and dns.lookup('localhost') or document the exact blocker
Write the results and any blockers into docs-internal/specs/upstream-node-runtime-plan.md or a linked preflight note
If any probe disproves the plan, record the exact blocker instead of guessing and stop short of broad implementation
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the explicit go/no-go story from the spec's pre-research gate. The purpose is to find out whether module_wrap, contextify, uv, and cares_wrap are bounded work or a design blocker.

userStories.title: Build standalone WasmVM backend probes for fs, sockets, DNS, and TTY
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need standalone WasmVM-targeted probes so we can verify that the backend operations we need fit the existing WebAssembly VM path.
acceptanceCriteria: Add standalone WasmVM-targeted probe programs for fs operations: open, read, write, stat, readdir, realpath
Add standalone WasmVM-targeted probe programs for socket operations: connect, listen, accept, read, write, close
Add standalone WasmVM-targeted probe programs for DNS operations and expected error paths
Add a standalone WasmVM-targeted probe for TTY/raw-mode behavior or document the exact missing kernel/libc piece
Compile the probes through the existing WasmVM path rather than through a custom one-off runtime
Add focused tests that run the probes through the sandbox and compare behavior to expected results
Document which backend operations fit cleanly into the existing WasmVM path and which need follow-up imports or ABI work
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These probes only validate backend operations. They do not prove module_wrap, contextify, JS handle identity, or callback/event delivery for tcp_wrap/stream_wrap.

userStories.title: Pin current LTS and add upstream Node asset sync scaffolding
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime pinned to the current Node LTS with deterministic asset sync tooling so we can rehost upstream assets without manual drift.
acceptanceCriteria: Record the chosen current LTS version and exact upstream commit in the new upstream asset metadata
Add a sync script such as scripts/sync-node-upstream-assets.ts that pulls assets from the pinned upstream fork checkout
Vendor the full pinned builtin JS tree required by the selected Node version into packages/nodejs/assets/upstream-node/
Add builtin-manifest and version metadata files that make the asset set reviewable and deterministic
Document expected fork/remotes and sync workflow in docs-internal/specs/upstream-node-runtime-plan.md
Focused tests or validation scripts pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do not cherry-pick a few builtin files. The sync flow should vendor the complete pinned builtin tree that the selected Node version needs.

userStories.title: Scaffold the upstream runtime source tree and asset loader
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the in-repo upstream runtime structure in place so later stories have a clean implementation surface.
acceptanceCriteria: Add the new upstream runtime directories under packages/nodejs/src/upstream/
Add an asset loader that can read the vendored upstream asset set and expose builtin sources/metadata to the runtime
Add initial runtime-driver/bootstrap-loader/internal-binding-registry scaffolding files with clear TODO boundaries
Keep the public NodeRuntime surface unified; use internal experimental entrypoints only where needed for probes and bring-up
Document the new layout in docs-internal/specs/upstream-node-runtime-plan.md if paths or responsibilities change
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story is structure only. It should not try to implement the full loader yet.

userStories.title: Implement builtins registry, compileFunction, and setInternalLoaders
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the Node builtins bootstrap contract implemented so upstream bootstrap code can construct internalBinding() and builtin require().
acceptanceCriteria: Implement an upstream builtin registry backed by the vendored manifest and source tree
Implement the builtins binding surface expected by internal/bootstrap/realm, including builtinIds, compileFunction, and setInternalLoaders
Compile builtin source with the correct Node-style wrapper signatures for the relevant bootstrap categories
Store the internal loaders produced by setInternalLoaders in runtime state for later builtin require() use
Add focused tests for builtin compilation, setInternalLoaders round-trip, and builtin cache behavior
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the first real bootstrap seam. If the current V8 runtime cannot support it from TypeScript alone, document and implement the minimum native/v8-runtime support instead of papering over it.

userStories.title: Boot internal/bootstrap/realm and internal/bootstrap/node in snapshot-free mode
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need Secure Exec to execute the upstream Node bootstrap path directly so we can prove the architecture before adding more module support.
acceptanceCriteria: Run internal/per_context/* and internal/bootstrap/realm successfully through the new upstream runtime path
Run internal/bootstrap/node successfully for a narrow entrypoint such as internal/main/eval_string
Keep the bring-up explicitly snapshot-free
Document the minimum bootstrap-critical binding set needed for the scoped bring-up
Add focused tests for bootstrap success in both standalone NodeRuntime and kernel-mounted runtime-driver mode
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do not broaden scope here. Worker threads, REPL, watch mode, inspector, and test-runner entrypoints remain out of scope.

userStories.title: Implement the minimal host binding registry for bootstrap and fs first-light
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need a host-side internalBinding registry for the early blockers so upstream bootstrap and fs can load without the legacy polyfill loader.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement the minimal host-side registry for bootstrap-critical bindings including builtins, module_wrap, contextify, config, util, process_methods, uv, cares_wrap, buffer, constants, errors, timers, and modules as needed
Classify each binding as host-only, host-lifecycle-plus-backend, or deferred
Keep JS-visible wrapper identity, callback delivery, ref/unref state, and close semantics on the host side
Document any deferred bindings explicitly instead of leaving implicit gaps
Add focused tests proving the registry supports the scoped bootstrap plus fs-first path
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story should not try to make every binding feature-complete. It should make the early blocker inventory explicit and working.

userStories.title: Make upstream fs the first real module target
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need upstream lib/fs.js to load and work through the new runtime so we can validate the architecture with a module that is large but still request/response-shaped.
acceptanceCriteria: require('fs') resolves through the new upstream runtime path rather than the old polyfill loader
Upstream fs sync APIs work for a narrow first-light subset: open/read/write/close/stat/readdir/realpath
Upstream callback-style fs APIs for the same subset work with correct callback delivery semantics
Add focused tests for upstream fs first-light in standalone and kernel-mounted modes
Document the exact fs v1 subset and the next deferred fs surfaces to implement
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: fs is the first real target after bootstrap. Keep the scope strict and explicit.

userStories.title: Compile a WasmVM-targeted backend artifact for fs bottom-half operations
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the fs backend compiled through the normal WasmVM path so the replacement runtime can start using the WebAssembly VM instead of growing more host bridge code.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a Node backend artifact that compiles through the existing WasmVM/WASIX toolchain rather than a one-off runtime path
Expose a versioned ABI for the fs bottom-half operations needed by upstream fs first-light
Instantiate and call the backend internally from the Node runtime rather than exposing it as a user-facing command
Keep JS-visible object identity and callback delivery on the host side while moving only the fs bottom-half operations into the backend
Add focused tests proving the backend artifact is used for fs first-light operations
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The backend should be a normal WasmVM-targeted artifact invoked internally. It is a sidecar service module, not a CLI command.

userStories.title: Expand fs parity across promises, streams, validation, and conformance
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need fs behavior expanded beyond first-light so we can trust the replacement runtime before broadening into other modules.
acceptanceCriteria: Expand upstream fs coverage to include fs.promises for the implemented subset
Add upstream fs stream coverage for the supported subset where it is realistic at this phase
Match Node-style validation and error behavior for the implemented fs APIs
Run the relevant vendored node-conformance fs coverage and remove stale expectations for newly passing tests
Regenerate packages/secure-exec/tests/node-conformance/conformance-report.json and docs/nodejs-conformance-report.mdx when expectations change
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do not jump to net/http before fs has a real confidence floor.

userStories.title: Replace the current polyfill/bootstrap Node path with the upstream runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the upstream runtime to become the canonical Node implementation so we stop maintaining the old path.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the old polyfill-first bootstrap path from the active Node implementation once bootstrap plus fs first-light are green
Make the upstream bootstrap path the canonical Node runtime path
Delete or retire legacy-only loader/polyfill code that is no longer part of the replacement architecture
Update docs-internal/specs/upstream-node-runtime-plan.md and any nearby architecture notes if implementation details changed materially
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The goal is replacement, not permanent dual compatibility. Do not remove the old path before bootstrap plus fs first-light are green on the new one.

userStories.title: Bring up net on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need net to work on the replacement runtime so we can start exercising the host-lifecycle versus Wasm-backend split for stream and socket handles.
acceptanceCriteria: require('net') resolves through the replacement runtime path
Minimal net server/client behavior works with upstream JS and the new binding split
Host-owned handle identity, callback/event delivery, and ref/unref behavior remain on the host side
Socket bottom-half operations run through the WasmVM-targeted backend where implemented
Add focused tests for net listen/connect/read/write/close behavior
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is where tcp_wrap/stream_wrap assumptions get real. Do not push JS handle identity into Wasm.

userStories.title: Bring up http and https on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need upstream http/https to work on the replacement runtime so we can validate the network stack above net.
acceptanceCriteria: require('http') and require('https') resolve through the replacement runtime path
Upstream http server/client round-trips work through the replacement runtime
Add wire-level differential tests that compare raw HTTP bytes against host Node for at least one representative scenario
Run the relevant vendored node-conformance coverage for the implemented http/https subset and remove stale expectations for newly passing tests
Regenerate packages/secure-exec/tests/node-conformance/conformance-report.json and docs/nodejs-conformance-report.mdx when expectations change
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do not hand-wave protocol correctness from same-code-path loopback tests alone.

userStories.title: Bring up readline and tty on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need upstream readline and tty behavior on the replacement runtime so interactive PTY workflows can start using the new implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: require('readline') resolves through the replacement runtime path
Basic readline line input works in a PTY-backed test
TTY raw mode, terminal size, and signal-sensitive behavior work for the supported subset
Add focused kernel-mediated tests for PTY/raw-mode behavior against the replacement runtime
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Out-of-scope runtime features should still fail deterministically without breaking bootstrap.

userStories.title: Implement modules binding and real package-json-aware ESM/CommonJS loading
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime to support the upstream modules binding and package JSON lookup paths so vendored tests and real packages can load from disk instead of only the current eval-string first-light path.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement the replacement-runtime internalBinding('modules') surface needed by internal/modules/package_json_reader and related loader paths, including getNearestParentPackageJSON or the exact current upstream equivalent
Replacement-runtime file execution supports a vendored conformance-style module that does relative require('../common') without falling back to the old runtime
Basic ESM entrypoints and relative import resolution work from real file paths on the replacement runtime
Add focused standalone and kernel-mounted tests that execute real files through the replacement runtime instead of only eval-string payloads
Run targeted vendored conformance files that previously failed in internal/modules/package_json_reader and remove stale expectations for any that now pass
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current issue: vendored conformance files that use relative require('../common') still hit missing internalBinding('modules').getNearestParentPackageJSON support. The replacement runtime needs a real modules binding and file-based loader path, not just eval-string first-light.

userStories.title: Replace bootstrap shims with bounded module_wrap, contextify, and async-hook runtime support
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime to stop depending on bootstrap-era shims for module_wrap, contextify, and async hook setup so ESM and vm behavior can progress beyond first-light.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit the current bootstrap shims used for module_wrap, contextify, buffer.setBufferPrototype(), and async_wrap.setupHooks() and document which are still temporary
Implement the minimum real runtime support needed for module_wrap and contextify to execute a trivial ESM module and a node:vm createContext()/runInContext() proof through the replacement runtime
If native/v8-runtime support is required, implement the smallest bounded change there instead of extending fake host-side shims indefinitely
internal/bootstrap/node no longer relies on a known-crash path in async_wrap.setupHooks() for the scoped replacement runtime bring-up
Add focused tests for trivial ESM import/export and node:vm context execution on the replacement runtime
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Critical issue encountered early: internal/bootstrap/node could abort in native async_wrap.setupHooks() before later scoped shims were added. This story is about replacing the bootstrap-only workaround with bounded runtime support.

userStories.title: Harden the helper-child execution protocol for buffered, async, and PTY modes
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime helper-child protocol to be explicit and reliable so async callbacks, plain node -e runs, and PTY sessions do not hang or silently lose output.
acceptanceCriteria: Define and document the process.__secureExecDone() contract and when the replacement runtime should and should not wait for it
Keep buffered JSON mode, async callback mode, and live-stdio/PTTY mode explicit instead of inferred by fragile heuristics
Strip inherited NODE_CHANNEL_FD and NODE_UNIQUE_ID from nested helper children where needed so forked test workers do not break output collection
Plain replacement-runtime node -e smoke paths complete without deadlocking in always-wait mode
Add focused tests for buffered exec, async callback completion, and PTY/live-stdio helper-child paths
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Issues encountered: always-wait mode can deadlock plain node -e runs; nested helpers spawned from forked workers can inherit NODE_CHANNEL_FD / NODE_UNIQUE_ID and silently stop producing buffered JSON output.

userStories.title: Finish fs FileHandle stream and WebStream parity on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the remaining fs FileHandle-owned stream surfaces to work on the replacement runtime so fs parity goes beyond the current first-light subset.
acceptanceCriteria: Build FileHandle-owned stream surfaces on top of backend-owned persistent fd/session state instead of the current path-reopen model
Implement the remaining backend fread or equivalent gap needed for FileHandle-owned createReadStream(), readableWebStream(), and related helpers
FileHandle-owned stream surfaces work on the replacement runtime for the supported subset instead of remaining deferred
Add focused tests for FileHandle createReadStream(), readableWebStream(), and any related FileHandle stream helpers that currently fail, including offset, append, and close behavior
Run the relevant vendored fs conformance coverage and remove stale expectations for any newly passing FileHandle stream cases
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Known unresolved fs issue from US-010: FileHandle-owned stream surfaces still fail on a backend fread gap and currently sit on top of a fake host-side fd table. This story assumes the preceding true backend fd/session work lands first.

userStories.title: Replace fs_event_wrap compatibility hacks with a real watcher strategy
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need a deliberate fs watcher strategy so vendored internal/fs/watchers can load without ad hoc prototype hacks and unsupported watcher APIs fail deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: Decide and document the full watcher strategy for the replacement runtime: real kernel/VFS event support or deterministic unsupported behavior across fs.watch(), fs.watchFile(), fs.promises.watch(), and recursive-watch variants
vendored internal/fs/watchers can load without relying on fragile one-off prototype subclass hacks in the bootstrap runner, or the unsupported path is made explicit and isolated
If watcher APIs remain unsupported, they fail with deterministic Node-style validation and unsupported-path behavior on the replacement runtime instead of half-loading and hanging
If watcher APIs are implemented, define the required kernel/VFS event primitive and wire fs_event_wrap host lifecycle on top of it
Add focused tests for watcher module loading and the chosen supported or unsupported behavior, including negative-path coverage for watchFile/fs.promises.watch
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Issue encountered during fs parity work: vendored internal/fs/watchers throws on the host FSEvent prototype unless the bootstrap runner hands it a fresh subclass. Contracts currently describe watcher APIs as deferred fail-fast behavior, so this story must either codify that cleanly on the replacement runtime or build the missing kernel primitive.

userStories.title: Replace net first-light hacks with a deliberate upstream network binding provider
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime net/http stack to move from helper-runner special cases to a real upstream binding provider that preserves host-owned handle identity and lifecycle semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the current helper-runner special cases and subclass hack for tcp_wrap.TCP owner alias with a deliberate binding-provider implementation

Add explicit upstream binding-registry coverage for the supported network bindings needed by the replacement runtime core, including tcp_wrap, stream_wrap, and http_parser, and document which adjacent bindings remain separate stories
Document and implement host-owned handle identity, callback/event delivery, ref/unref semantics, and close behavior for the supported network bindings on the replacement runtime
Keep only the socket bottom-half operations in the Wasm backend; do not move JS handle identity into Wasm
Add focused tests for repeated read/write events, close ordering, parser/wire behavior, and any net timeout/ref-unref behavior included in the supported subset
Run targeted vendored net conformance coverage and remove stale expectations for any newly passing cases
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Known issue from US-012: vendored lib/net.js currently needs a subclassed tcp_wrap.TCP so it can redefine the legacy owner alias, and the bootstrap runner still falls back to host internalBinding() for most network bindings. This story is about migrating the supported network path onto the explicit upstream binding registry.

userStories.title: Expand http parity beyond first-light with agent, timeout, abort, and protocol edge cases
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime HTTP stack to move beyond first-light round-trips into the edge cases that real packages and vendored Node tests rely on.
acceptanceCriteria: Keep the whole vendored HTTP family together on the replacement runtime and avoid mixing pinned http.js with host _http_* or host internal/http helpers
Implement or harden keepalive/agent reuse, keep-alive timeout/header behavior, abort ordering, and upgrade/CONNECT handling on the replacement runtime
Add or extend wire-level differential tests that compare raw HTTP bytes against host Node for representative parser and protocol edge cases, not just one happy path
Run targeted vendored http conformance coverage for the implemented subset and remove stale expectations for any newly passing cases
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Known issue from US-013: HTTP first-light is stable only when the entire HTTP family stays vendored together. Next work is protocol and lifecycle parity for upgrades, parser edges, timeout headers, and abort behavior, not just another happy-path round-trip.

userStories.title: Bring up direct tls/tls_wrap behavior and finish https integration on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime to handle real direct tls flows so https stops being only a module-resolution proof layered over host TLS behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Replacement-runtime tls.connect(), tls.createServer(), and TLSSocket support a real TLS-backed round-trip for at least one representative scenario

Document and implement the minimum tls_wrap/TLSSocket behavior needed for the supported subset, including authorization state and at least one ALPN, SNI, certificate, or session-related behavior that matters for real usage
Replacement-runtime https requests run through the replacement tls path rather than only host-side module resolution proof
Add focused tests for direct tls and https requests on the replacement runtime, including at least one certificate/agent-related behavior that matters for real usage
Run targeted vendored https/tls conformance coverage for the implemented subset and remove stale expectations for any newly passing cases
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current state from US-013 is https module resolution plus shared HTTP-family wiring. There is not yet direct replacement-runtime coverage for tls.createServer(), tls.connect(), or TLSSocket lifecycle, so this story has to make TLS first-class rather than hiding it inside https.

userStories.title: Promote cares_wrap and dns behavior from probe-only to replacement-runtime parity
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need DNS behavior on the replacement runtime to move beyond preflight probes so packages and vendored tests can rely on it.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement the supported cares_wrap-backed DNS behavior needed by the replacement runtime beyond the existing probe-only lookup proof, including dns.lookup(), dns.promises, Resolver, and resolve4/resolve6 for the chosen subset
Document and implement default result-order behavior and any autoselect-family interactions that the supported HTTP/net subset depends on
Decide and document the supported or unsupported behavior for lookupService(), getServers()/setServers(), and any remaining resolve* APIs rather than leaving them as implicit gaps
Add focused tests for localhost lookup, external lookup, resolve4/resolve6, Resolver usage, result-order behavior, and representative error cases on the replacement runtime
Add any minimal vendored conformance helper shims required under packages/secure-exec/tests/node-conformance/common/ for DNS fixtures instead of rewriting vendored tests
Run targeted vendored dns conformance coverage for the implemented subset and remove stale expectations for any newly passing cases
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The pre-research gate proved dns.lookup() at a probe level, but the replacement runtime still needs a real cares_wrap/dns strategy instead of treating the probe as parity. Richer DNS parity is likely to depend on the separate udp_wrap/dgram bring-up.

userStories.title: Align readline/TTY with real WasmVM raw-mode backend support
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime's readline and TTY behavior to line up with real WasmVM/kernel raw-mode support rather than relying only on helper-child PTY control paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Define and implement the missing WasmVM/libc/kernel TTY ABI needed for raw mode, including the required host imports and libc plumbing for terminal attributes and window size, or document the exact remaining platform gap
Replacement-runtime readline/TTY behavior uses a deliberate raw-mode backend story instead of only the current helper-child control-path workaround

Add a standalone WasmVM-targeted TTY/raw-mode probe once the ABI exists, plus focused kernel-mediated tests for raw mode, terminal geometry, and signal-sensitive readline behavior that cover the supported subset
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Known issue from US-002 and US-014: TTY raw-mode backend support was still blocked at the WasmVM/libc/kernel layer during probe work, while the replacement runtime first-light used a helper-child PTY strategy. The current WasmVM ABI only proves isatty-style detection, not tcgetattr/tcsetattr/winsize parity.

userStories.title: Run a replacement-runtime conformance sweep for fs, modules, net, http, https, dns, and readline
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the vendored conformance suite rerun against the replacement runtime so we stop relying on first-light proofs alone.
acceptanceCriteria: Run the relevant vendored node-conformance coverage for the replacement runtime across fs, modules/ESM, net, http, https, dns, and readline/tty
When a vendored file fails on a missing ../common/* helper, add the minimal shim under packages/secure-exec/tests/node-conformance/common/ instead of rewriting the vendored test
Remove stale expectations.json entries for tests that now pass on the replacement runtime
Remaining failures have specific, accurate reasons tied to the replacement runtime gaps rather than vague placeholders
Regenerate packages/secure-exec/tests/node-conformance/conformance-report.json and docs/nodejs-conformance-report.mdx
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The current log proves first-light milestones. This story is the parity sweep that turns those milestones into an honest conformance picture.

userStories.title: Add project-matrix fixtures that exercise the replacement runtime through real packages
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need black-box project fixtures that use fs, ESM/modules, net, http, https, and readline so we can compare the replacement runtime to host Node on real package behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Add or update project-matrix fixtures under packages/secure-exec/tests/projects/ that exercise the replacement runtime through real package code rather than synthetic harness-only examples

Cover at least fs, module loading/ESM, one HTTP client fixture such as axios or node-fetch, one upgrade/TLS-sensitive fixture such as ws, one server fixture such as express or fastify, and one interactive or readline-sensitive fixture where realistic
Keep fixtures sandbox-blind and compare normalized code/stdout/stderr against host Node
Update docs/nodejs-compatibility.mdx or the tested-package tracking if new fixtures are added
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The replacement runtime is beyond pure research now. The next confidence layer after conformance is real package behavior through the project matrix, including at least one HTTP client fixture, one upgrade/TLS-sensitive fixture, and one server fixture that all exercise the public replacement path.

userStories.title: Implement a real process_methods provider for the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the upstream runtime to stop carrying process bootstrap through planned or partial process_methods behavior so internal/process, warning, stdio, and process-state paths can execute without hidden legacy assumptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the current planned-only internalBinding('process_methods') inventory entry with a deliberate provider for the replacement runtime rather than relying on bootstrap-era assumptions
Document which process_methods surface is required for bootstrap, process warnings, stdio reset/testing hooks, and process-state switches, and which parts remain intentionally unsupported

Add focused tests that execute the supported process_methods-backed behaviors through the replacement runtime, including at least one internal/bootstrap path and one public process-facing path that currently depends on the shimmed state
Run targeted vendored conformance coverage that touches process bootstrap modules or process_methods-dependent helpers and remove stale expectations for any newly passing cases
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current gap: the upstream binding inventory still marks process_methods as planned even though vendored internal/bootstrap/node.js and internal/process/* depend on it. This is a real missing provider, not just bookkeeping.

userStories.title: Implement fs_dir, opendir, and Dir wrapper parity on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime to support upstream internal/fs/dir.js and public opendir/Dir APIs so fs parity includes host-owned directory handles instead of stopping at path-based readdir and streams.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement the replacement-runtime internalBinding('fs_dir') provider needed by upstream internal/fs/dir.js
Support the public fs.opendir(), fs.promises.opendir(), Dir.read(), Dir.readSync(), Dir.close(), and async iterator surfaces for the supported subset
Keep Dir/Dirent wrapper identity and close lifecycle on the host side while routing directory bottom-half operations through the chosen backend boundary
Add focused tests for opendir success, ENOTDIR/error paths, Dir iteration, and Dir close semantics on the replacement runtime
Run targeted vendored fs opendir/readdir-dirent conformance coverage and remove stale expectations for any newly passing cases
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The upstream binding inventory still marks fs_dir as deferred, and current fs parity work only covered path-based readdir plus FileHandle/path streams. Dir wrappers are a separate missing host-lifecycle surface.

userStories.title: Expand node:module builtin parity for createRequire, package lookup, compile cache, and source-map APIs
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime's node:module surface to handle real package workflows and vendored module API tests instead of stopping at the minimal loader path.
acceptanceCriteria: Support the replacement-runtime node:module behaviors needed by real packages and targeted vendored tests, including createRequire() and package lookup paths that operate on real files instead of eval-only entrypoints

Decide and document the supported or explicitly unsupported behavior for compile-cache and source-map-related module APIs such as enableCompileCache(), getCompileCacheDir(), setSourceMapsSupport(), getSourceMapsSupport(), and findSourceMap()
If any node:module APIs remain unsupported, make them fail deterministically with Node-style validation and unsupported-path behavior rather than silent omission
Add focused tests for createRequire() from ESM, package-json lookup, and the chosen compile-cache/source-map behavior on the replacement runtime
Run targeted vendored conformance coverage for node:module API files and remove stale expectations for any newly passing cases
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The current PRD covers modules binding and file-based loading, but vendored node:module API coverage goes further than getNearestParentPackageJSON. Compile-cache and source-map APIs are visible gaps in the current replacement-runtime story.

userStories.title: Reconcile target Node version governance with the pinned upstream runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the repo's target-version policy, docs, contracts, and validation baseline to agree on which Node line Secure Exec now targets so replacement-runtime work is measured against a single truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Resolve the mismatch between the pinned upstream runtime assets and the repo's current target Node version statements in docs and contracts

Either upgrade the validation baseline and related package/tooling references to the chosen target line or explicitly document why the pinned upstream runtime intentionally diverges and how compatibility is measured
Update the affected governance and compatibility documents to describe the same target line consistently
Add focused validation or documentation checks that fail if the declared target version and the replacement-runtime pin drift apart again
Focused tests or checks pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current issue: the replacement runtime is pinned to an upstream Node 24 release while docs/contracts still declare a 22.x target derived from the @types/node baseline. That inconsistency will poison every parity claim until it is resolved.

userStories.title: Refresh compatibility docs, architecture docs, and contracts for the upstream runtime model
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the public and internal documentation to describe the replacement upstream runtime instead of the old bridge/polyfill model so contributors and users can reason about the actual architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: Update docs/nodejs-compatibility.mdx to describe the current upstream-runtime architecture, supported/deferred/unsupported module tiers, and Tested Packages table accurately
Update docs-internal/arch/overview.md to remove stale legacy-only descriptions and reflect the current NodeRuntime/createNodeRuntime replacement architecture

Update the relevant contracts under .agent/contracts/ so node-stdlib, node-runtime, compatibility-governance, and documentation requirements match the upstream-runtime model instead of the old polyfill-first assumptions
Where behavior remains intentionally unsupported or deferred, document the exact replacement-runtime contract rather than leaving old bridge/polyfill text in place
Focused docs/contract checks pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The current docs and contracts still describe the old bridge/polyfill runtime. For example, the compatibility page still marks net/tls/readline as deferred and describes a bridge/polyfill matrix that no longer matches the public replacement path.

userStories.title: Add dev-shell proof paths for replacement-runtime fs, ESM, http, and readline workflows
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the canonical dev shell to prove the replacement runtime end to end so manual validation and automated shell coverage exercise more than node -e version checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Add focused dev-shell coverage that runs replacement-runtime workflows for at least fs, a real file-based ESM/module path, one http or https round-trip, and one readline or PTY-sensitive interaction
Keep the validation entirely inside the sandbox-native dev shell path with no host-backed fallback commands or overrides
Where an interactive path is required, exercise it through kernel.openShell() and the dev-shell PTY harness rather than a host PTY
Document any remaining dev-shell-visible gaps explicitly if a workflow cannot yet be validated end to end
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current dev-shell coverage proves the shell boots and node can print process.version, but it does not yet give us an honest manual-validation path for the new upstream fs/module/http/readline surfaces.

userStories.title: Add package and CI guards for vendored upstream assets and replacement-runtime backend artifacts
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need packaging and CI checks that prove the replacement runtime ships its vendored Node assets and Wasm backend artifacts correctly so parity work does not regress at publish or build time.
acceptanceCriteria: Add focused checks that the published @secure-exec/nodejs package includes the vendored upstream asset tree, replacement bootstrap scripts, and backend artifacts required by the replacement runtime
Add or update CI-facing artifact availability checks so missing replacement-runtime Wasm backend assets fail clearly before runtime tests start
Codify the required build or refresh steps for replacement-runtime artifacts so changes to upstream assets or backend ABIs cannot silently pass local source tests while shipped packages break
Document the packaging/build guard flow in the relevant package or internal docs
Focused tests or checks pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The replacement runtime now depends on vendored upstream assets, helper scripts, and packaged backend binaries. Right now that packaging contract is only partially enforced through story-specific checks and progress notes.

userStories.title: Move replacement-runtime execution ownership back under NodeExecutionDriver and secure-exec-v8
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime to execute through the actual Secure Exec V8 runtime instead of a host `node --expose-internals` helper so sandbox guarantees and runtime budgets still apply.
acceptanceCriteria: For supported replacement-runtime exec and kernel-mounted node paths, execution no longer depends on spawning process.execPath with --expose-internals as the primary runtime mechanism
Standalone and kernel-mounted replacement-runtime execution preserve Secure Exec runtime controls such as memoryLimit, cpuTimeLimitMs, timingMitigation, and isolate recovery after timeout
If a narrow host-Node helper remains for preflight or asset-validation purposes, it is explicitly internal-only and not the product execution path
Add focused tests that prove replacement-runtime execution still satisfies existing timeout, isolate-recovery, and timing-mitigation contracts on the new path
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current critical gap: packages/nodejs/src/upstream/bootstrap-execution.ts still powers the public replacement runtime by spawning process.execPath with --expose-internals. That bypasses the real secure-exec-v8 execution path and risks invalidating memory/cpu/timing guarantees.

userStories.title: Restore filePath-aware exec and run() export semantics on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime to honor the NodeRuntime exec/run contract for file-backed CommonJS and ESM modules instead of treating everything as eval-only code.
acceptanceCriteria: Replacement-runtime exec(code, { filePath }) uses the provided filePath for module classification, __filename/__dirname, and relative resolution instead of hardcoding [eval]
Replacement-runtime run(code, filePath) returns module exports for CommonJS and namespace exports for ESM in line with the NodeRuntime contract
Relative require('./x') and import './x' from run() and file-backed exec() resolve from the provided parent file path on the replacement runtime
Add focused tests covering CommonJS exports, ESM exports, and file-backed relative loading on the replacement runtime using the existing runtime-driver/node contract surface
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current critical gap: ExperimentalUpstreamBootstrapRuntimeDriver.run() ignores filePath and never returns exports, and the helper runner compiles user code with filename '[eval]'. That breaks the published NodeRuntime contract and a large slice of existing runtime-driver tests.

userStories.title: Replace host require fallback with sandboxed dependency and package resolution on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime to resolve third-party packages and filesystem modules through the sandbox VFS/module-access layer rather than falling back to host require().
acceptanceCriteria: Non-builtin specifiers on the replacement runtime resolve through sandboxed module resolution and ModuleAccessFileSystem/projected package overlays rather than HOST_REQUIRE(specifier)

Support the package-resolution behaviors required by real packages and contracts, including package.json exports/imports conditions, require.resolve()/require.resolve.paths(), createRequire() parent semantics, and pnpm virtual-store target resolution for supported paths
Sandboxed absolute and realpath-derived package asset paths that canonicalize inside the configured overlay continue to resolve as projected package assets rather than escaping to the host runtime
Missing third-party packages fail with deterministic sandbox module-resolution errors instead of silently loading from the host environment
Add focused tests using real package-style layouts under the sandbox filesystem to prove the replacement runtime no longer depends on host require fallbacks
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current critical gap: the replacement bootstrap runner still routes non-builtin requires and require.resolve() through HOST_REQUIRE. That violates the node-bridge and node-stdlib contracts and makes package parity claims untrustworthy.

userStories.title: Promote async_hooks and AsyncLocalStorage from bootstrap no-ops to supported replacement-runtime behavior
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need user-facing async_hooks behavior on the replacement runtime so frameworks relying on AsyncLocalStorage and async context propagation can run without the current stub/no-op semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Move the replacement runtime beyond the current async_wrap/setupHooks bootstrap no-op by implementing the supported async_hooks and AsyncLocalStorage subset deliberately
Async context propagation works across the supported replacement-runtime async boundaries, including timers and at least one supported net/http path
Document which async_hooks surfaces are supported, deferred, or intentionally unsupported on the replacement runtime instead of leaving the module as a vague stub
Add focused tests for AsyncLocalStorage, AsyncResource, and createHook behavior on the supported subset
Run targeted vendored async_hooks / async-local-storage conformance coverage and remove stale expectations for any newly passing cases
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current gap: US-016 only covers bounded bootstrap/runtime support for async hooks. The public async_hooks module is still effectively stubbed in docs and conformance, which will block real framework parity even if bootstrap succeeds.

userStories.title: Implement pipe_wrap and AF_UNIX path-socket parity on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime to support pipe_wrap and Unix path sockets so upstream net, child-process, and tls-related path-socket flows do not stall on TCP-only first-light support.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement the replacement-runtime internalBinding('pipe_wrap') provider needed by upstream net, child_process, and tls helpers for the supported subset
Replacement-runtime net server.listen(path), net.connect(path), and related AF_UNIX path-socket flows work through the kernel socket layer instead of falling back to TCP-only behavior
readableAll and writableAll path-listener options update the created socket-file mode bits and abstract-unix-socket validation follows Node-compatible behavior for the supported subset
Add focused tests for pipe_wrap handle ref/unref/close semantics and AF_UNIX path-socket round-trips on the replacement runtime
Run targeted vendored conformance coverage for path-socket and pipe-wrap-sensitive files and remove stale expectations for any newly passing cases
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current gap: the first-light net story calls out tcp_wrap and stream_wrap, but upstream net.js and related internals also depend on pipe_wrap and AF_UNIX behavior. Path sockets are covered by kernel contracts but not yet explicitly tracked in the replacement-runtime PRD.

userStories.title: Retire supported-subset host fallback and host-context builtin hacks from the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime to stop depending on host-context builtin execution and supported-subset fallback shims so parity claims are based on the actual replacement loader and bindings.
acceptanceCriteria: For the supported replacement-runtime subset, remove reliance on public-builtin-host-fallback and host-context builtin execution as the normal path for vendored builtins

Shrink or eliminate supported-subset bootstrap shims such as http-cluster-host-context, tcp_wrap-owner-subclass, and fs_event_wrap-fsevent-subclass where real providers now exist; any remaining shim must be explicitly documented as a temporary bounded exception
Focused tests and first-light reports show the supported replacement-runtime subset running without unsupported host fallback paths
Document any intentionally retained host-side fallback as an explicit temporary boundary rather than leaving it hidden in appliedBindingShims
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current gap: packages/nodejs/scripts/run-upstream-node-bootstrap.mjs still advertises appliedBindingShims like public-builtin-host-fallback, http-cluster-host-context, tcp_wrap-owner-subclass, and fs_event_wrap-fsevent-subclass. Those were useful for first-light, but they are not a stable replacement-runtime end state.

userStories.title: Replace the fake host-side fd table with a true backend fd/session ABI
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime fs backend to own real file-descriptor/session state so open-file semantics match Node instead of reopening paths for every operation.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the current host-invented fd table and path-reopen model with a backend-owned fd/session ABI that preserves open-handle semantics across read, write, seek, stat, truncate, and close operations
Backend operations address persistent handles rather than reopening by pathname for every read/write call
Add focused tests for rename-after-open, unlink-after-open, append/seek correctness, close ordering, and other fd-lifetime cases that the current path-reopen model cannot satisfy
Document the fd/session ABI and how host-owned FileHandle and FSReqCallback lifecycle maps onto it
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current gap: packages/nodejs/scripts/upstream-node-fs-binding.mjs invents numeric fds in JS while native/wasmvm/c/programs/node_fs_backend.c reopens by path on each operation. That is not sufficient for real FileHandle or inode-lifetime parity.

userStories.title: Expand fs metadata and mutation parity beyond the current backend ABI subset
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime fs layer to cover the remaining metadata and mutation APIs that real packages and vendored tests exercise, not just open/read/write/stat/readdir/realpath.
acceptanceCriteria: Expand the replacement-runtime fs binding and backend support for the chosen subset of metadata and mutation APIs, including mkdir/rmdir, truncate/ftruncate, utimes/futimes, chmod/chown, symlink/readlink/link, and related validation or umask behavior where supported
Match Node-style validation and error behavior for the implemented fs metadata and mutation APIs on the replacement runtime
Add focused tests for the implemented APIs across standalone and kernel-mounted replacement-runtime paths
Run targeted vendored fs conformance coverage for the implemented metadata and mutation subset and remove stale expectations for any newly passing cases
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current backend ABI only covers open, read, write, stat, lstat, readdir, and realpath. A large set of public fs mutation and metadata behavior is still outside the replacement-runtime backlog unless it is called out explicitly.

userStories.title: Bring up dgram and udp_wrap on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime to support UDP sockets so DNS, dgram, and related networking features stop depending on probe-only behavior or host fallbacks.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement the replacement-runtime binding and provider support needed for udp_wrap and the supported dgram subset
Support the chosen dgram subset through the kernel-mediated UDP path rather than host-side shortcuts
Add focused tests for bind/send/receive/close behavior and representative error paths on the replacement runtime
Run targeted vendored dgram conformance coverage for the implemented subset and remove stale expectations for any newly passing cases
Document how udp_wrap and dgram interact with the DNS parity plan
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current upstream replacement runtime has no udp_wrap handling at all. Richer DNS parity and multi-channel resolution work are likely to stall until UDP exists as a first-class runtime surface.
